title: Clickhous CLI Client: UPDATE/INSERT does not respect \ (backslashes)
description:
### Company or project name
langfuse
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
Insert / Update statements on `String` columns don't preserve backslashes.
### Which ClickHouse versions are affected?
```
ClickHouse client version 25.12.1.649 (official build).
Connected to ClickHouse server version 25.8.13.
```
### How to reproduce
```
ClickHouse client version 25.12.1.649 (official build).
Connected to ClickHouse server version 25.8.13.
```
then,
```sql
CREATE TABLE hi ( id String, lol Nullable(String) CODEC(ZSTD(3)) ) ENGINE = MergeTree ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO hi (id, lol)VALUES ('one', 'haha');
ALTER TABLE hi UPDATE lol = '{"messages":"[{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"I want' WHERE id = 'one';
```
output is:
```shell
ALTER TABLE default.hi
(UPDATE lol = '{"messages":"[{"role":"user","content":"I want' WHERE id = 'one')
```
As you can see, the `\` are GONE.
If I hex the values and update with `lol = unhex($HEXED VALUES)` , the `\` are correctly preserved.
### Expected behavior
The backslashes are exactly retained as I input them.
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
_No response_
**EDIT**: this is probably a **bug** not **unexpected behaviour**.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table ( id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, description VARCHAR NULL );
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, description) VALUES (1, 'test\\name', 'This is a test\\description');
UPDATE test_table SET description = '{"info":"This is a test with a backslash: \\\"}' WHERE id = 1;
CREATE TABLE another_table ( id INTEGER, value VARCHAR DEFAULT 'default\\value' );
key_bug_features: UPDATE/INSERT statements lose backslashes in String columns
